title: Reconcile hook timeout defaults: RLM_TIMEOUT 60s exceeds documented 30s hook timeout
description:
The hook's internal timeout default is 60s (CONFIG.timeout, RLM_TIMEOUT, rlm-hook.mjs ~line 37; also documented in README.md Configuration), but both README.md (Installation section settings.json snippet) and install.sh (printed settings snippet) tell users to register the hook with "timeout": 30. Claude Code will kill the hook at 30s, before the hook's own 60s SIGTERM/SIGKILL handling (invokeHaiku, ~line 876) ever fires — so on slow agentic runs the graceful-degradation path (exit 0, no output) is bypassed by an external kill.

Fix (pick one and make all three places consistent):
a) raise the snippet timeout in README.md and install.sh to a value above RLM_TIMEOUT (e.g. 90), or
b) lower the RLM_TIMEOUT default below the snippet's 30s (e.g. 25000ms), or
c) document explicitly that RLM_TIMEOUT must be set below the hooks timeout and choose coherent defaults.

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md snippet, install.sh snippet, and CONFIG.timeout default are mutually coherent (internal timeout strictly less than registered hook timeout)
- README Latency profile / Configuration sections reflect the chosen values
- No test regressions: node --test test/unit.test.mjs green

title: Add missing LICENSE file (MIT)
description:
README.md declares 'MIT. See LICENSE.' with a relative link to LICENSE, and package.json sets license: MIT, but no LICENSE file exists in the repo root. The link 404s on the public mirror.

Fix: add a standard MIT LICENSE file at the repo root with copyright holder jedarden and the current year.

Acceptance criteria: LICENSE exists at repo root, contains the standard MIT text, README link resolves.

title: Pid-scope the CLI agentic scratch file to prevent concurrent-run collisions
description:
Plan Open Question 2 (docs/plan/plan.md, Open Questions section) flags that the fixed scratch path .claude/rlm-scratch.md can collide. The SDK agentic path already resolved this with a pid-scoped file (sdkScratchPath in rlm-hook.mjs, ~line 1167: .claude/rlm-scratch-<pid>.md), but the SUBPROCESS agentic path did not: buildRLMPrompt (rlm-hook.mjs ~line 716) hardcodes scratchFile = '.claude/rlm-scratch.md' into the Haiku prompt, and main() (~line 1808) cleans up that same fixed path in a finally block. Two concurrent hook invocations (user submits prompts rapidly — plan Open Question 5) will overwrite each other's notes, and one run's cleanup can delete the other run's scratch mid-exploration.

Fix: use a pid-scoped scratch filename in buildRLMPrompt for the agentic subprocess prompt (mirror sdkScratchPath naming), and have main() clean up that same pid-scoped path. Keep the Bash(rm:*) allowedTools entry working for Haiku's self-cleanup step.

Acceptance criteria:
- buildRLMPrompt no longer embeds the fixed '.claude/rlm-scratch.md' path; the path includes process.pid (or equivalent unique token)
- main()'s subprocess-path finally cleanup removes the same pid-scoped file
- Unit tests in test/unit.test.mjs (Group 9, RLM Prompt Building) updated to assert the pid-scoped path appears in the agentic prompt
- node --test test/unit.test.mjs stays green

title: SDK mode unusable from installed hook location — document or fix dependency resolution
description:
install.sh copies only rlm-hook.mjs and rlm-hook.sh to ~/.claude/hooks/. The Phase 2 SDK path lazily imports @anthropic-ai/sdk (createAnthropicClient, rlm-hook.mjs approx line 939), but Node module resolution from ~/.claude/hooks/ will not find the SDK installed in the repo checkout's node_modules — so for a normally-installed hook, RLM_USE_SDK=true silently falls back to the subprocess path on every run (the import throws, the catch logs and degrades). Users following README get a feature that cannot activate. Also note plan.md Phase 2 said 'Add @anthropic-ai/sdk as optional dependency' but package.json lists it under dependencies.

Fix options (choose one):
a) install.sh additionally installs the SDK next to the hook (npm install --prefix ~/.claude/hooks @anthropic-ai/sdk) when the user opts into SDK mode,
b) resolve the SDK relative to the source repo (e.g. try import from a configurable RLM_SDK_PATH), or
c) document clearly in README that SDK mode requires running the hook from the repo checkout (point settings.json at the checkout path instead of ~/.claude/hooks/) and move the SDK to optionalDependencies per the plan.

Acceptance criteria: a user can enable RLM_USE_SDK=true following only the README and observe the SDK path being used (log line 'Used SDK-Direct ... path' in the rlm-hook log); package.json dependency classification matches the plan or the plan is amended; install.sh and README agree.

title: Semantic cache hygiene: clean up orphaned .embedding sidecars and stale index.json entries
description:
Plan Phase 3 (docs/plan/plan.md) explicitly lists 'Index needs compaction as cache grows' as a tradeoff, but no compaction was implemented. In rlm-hook.mjs, checkCache (approx line 131) deletes only the expired <key>.json cache entry; the companion <key>.embedding sidecar (written by saveCacheEmbedding, approx line 1416) and the key's inline vector in index.json (updateCacheIndex, approx line 1386) are never removed. Consequences: the cache dir grows without bound with orphaned float32 sidecars; semanticLookup (approx line 1533) keeps scoring stale vectors on every SHA miss (scoreFromIndex and scoreFromFiles both include them), paying I/O and cosine cost for entries that then resolve to 'entry gone' misses.

Fix (best-effort, gated like the rest of the semantic layer so failures never break the hook):
- when checkCache expires a <key>.json, also rm the sibling <key>.embedding (force) and drop the key from index.json
- and/or on 'semantic match but entry gone' in semanticLookup, delete that orphan embedding and prune the index entry so it never re-matches

Acceptance criteria: after a cache entry expires, its .embedding sidecar and index.json entry are removed on the next access path that discovers the expiry; a corrupt or missing index still degrades to the file scan; new unit tests in test/unit.test.mjs cover the orphan-cleanup path; existing groups 14-18 stay green.

title: Refresh README.md and CLAUDE.md to match shipped Phases 2-5
description:
README.md and CLAUDE.md document only the Phase 1 surface and have drifted from the code (rlm-hook.mjs). Concrete mismatches:

1. README Configuration section is missing all Phase 2-5 env vars defined in CONFIG (rlm-hook.mjs approx lines 32-76): RLM_USE_SDK, RLM_SDK_MAX_TOKENS (+ ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), RLM_SEMANTIC_CACHE, RLM_SEMANTIC_THRESHOLD, RLM_EMBED_MODEL, RLM_EMBED_BASE_URL (+ OPENAI_API_KEY), RLM_METRICS_FILE, RLM_CONTEXT_WINDOW, RLM_GATHER_CONTEXT.
2. README 'Example output' shows an XML-element structure (nested file elements etc.) that formatOutput (approx line 1601) never emits — the real output is pretty-printed JSON inside rlm_preresearch tags followed by a plain-text PRERESEARCH COMPLETE summary. Replace with a truthful example.
3. README Development section lists 'npm run lint' but package.json has no lint script — either add a lint script or drop the line.
4. README says nothing about the metrics dashboard (bench/dashboard.mjs --serve, port 9876) or Phase 4 context reuse; a short section for each would surface the shipped features.
5. CLAUDE.md documents an RLM_MODE env var (agentic|fast|detailed) that does not exist — the code uses RLM_AGENTIC_MODE and RLM_FAST_MODE. CLAUDE.md also says the hook is '664 lines, no external deps currently' (it is ~1880 lines with @anthropic-ai/sdk as a dependency) and frames Phase 2 as future ('Phase 2 adds the SDK').
6. README skip-detection table lists patterns (cargo build, bare question words) that only skip via the min-length rule, not dedicated patterns — align the table with shouldSkipRLM (approx line 159).

Acceptance criteria: every documented env var exists in CONFIG and vice versa; the example output matches what formatOutput actually prints; no references to nonexistent npm scripts or env vars; CLAUDE.md line counts and dependency claims accurate.

title: Update plan.md: mark Phases 3-5 complete, record resolved open questions
description:
docs/plan/plan.md is stale relative to the shipped code. Phase 3 (Semantic Caching), Phase 4 (Conversation Context Awareness), and Phase 5 (Metrics Dashboard) are all marked '[ ]' but are fully implemented and tested: semantic cache (embedText, cosineSimilarity, saveCacheEmbedding, semanticLookup, plus the index.json reverse index — rlm-hook.mjs approx lines 1270-1566; test groups 14-18), Phase 4 (extractPriorRLMBlocks, extractPriorBlocksFromTranscript, classifyIntentLocal, computeChangedFiles, findReusablePriorBlockWithMtime, and the main() early-exit — approx lines 310-677 and 1778-1804; test groups 19-23), Phase 5 (appendMetric and recordMetric wired at every main() exit, bench/parse-log.mjs, bench/dashboard.mjs; test groups 24-26). .marathon/STATE.md and .marathon/DONE confirm all five phases complete with 272 unit + 15 integration tests.

Update plan.md to:
- Mark Phases 3, 4, 5 as COMPLETE [x] with brief deliverable notes (mirroring the Phase 1 and Phase 2 style)
- Update the Open Questions section: Q2 (scratch file location) is resolved for the SDK path (pid-scoped scratch file) but still open for the CLI subprocess path; Phase 4's block-timestamp question was resolved (per-record ts stamping, option a); Phase 3's index design decision (inline vectors in index.json, file scan fallback) should be recorded
- Note deviations from the original spec worth keeping: metrics event taxonomy extended beyond the four planned events (adds semantic source, context_reuse, haiku_skip, complete); embeddings use an OpenAI-compatible endpoint keyed by OPENAI_API_KEY

Acceptance criteria: plan.md phase checkboxes match reality; open questions section distinguishes resolved vs still-open; no code changes.
